episode_summary: The team discuss a world suddenly without bacteria, Moldova’s vast wine cellars, the royal family’s 1917 rebrand to “Windsor” and other surname quirks, and an audacious theft of an entire Czech ski lift alongside other remarkable heists. Lively tangents range from wine history and cellars to naming conventions and large-scale thefts.

facts.fact: Okay, my fact this week is that if all the bacteria in the world disappeared, we wouldn't notice for about a week.
My fact is the world's largest wine cellar has tunnels 150 miles long, and it's so big it has to have traffic rules for people who drive through it.
Yep, my fact this week is that, before they settled on the name Windsor, surnames that were considered for the royal family, included gulf, whipper, wetting, chewed a steward, and England.
My fact of this week is that in 2012, thieves stole an entire ski lift from the Czech Republic.
